description: Fix 5 objective defects identified during a full-platform audit: scaffold display name generation, silent adapter hash mismatch, flaky E2E diagnostic sampling, empty wsUrl validation, and silent sync notification failures.

userStories.title: Fix scaffold display name to use title case instead of naive capitalization
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolded plugin's displayName to use proper title case so that the side panel and tool descriptions show a polished name like 'My Plugin' instead of 'My-plugin'.
acceptanceCriteria: displayName fallback on line 173 of scaffold.ts produces space-separated title case: 'my-cool-plugin' → 'My Cool Plugin'
displayName fallback on line 203 of scaffold.ts uses the same logic
The toPascalCase function on line 166 is NOT changed (it is correct for class names)
A new helper like toTitleCase is used or the logic is inlined consistently in both locations
Existing unit tests in platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts still pass
If no test covers displayName generation, add one that verifies 'my-cool-plugin' → 'My Cool Plugin'
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts lines 173 and 203. Current code: `args.name.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + args.name.slice(1)` which for 'my-plugin' produces 'My-plugin'. The fix is to split on hyphens, capitalize each word, and join with spaces: `args.name.split('-').map(p => p.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + p.slice(1)).join(' ')`. This produces human-readable display names ('My Plugin') while the class name continues to use toPascalCase ('MyPlugin'). Extract a toTitleCase helper next to toPascalCase for clarity. Both line 173 and line 203 must be updated identically.

userStories.title: Throw error on adapter hash mismatch after retry instead of silent warning
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want adapter injection to fail loudly when the adapter hash doesn't match after a retry so that I can diagnose broken plugin injections instead of seeing phantom tool availability.
acceptanceCriteria: After the retry on line 144 of iife-injection.ts, if retryMatched is false, the function throws an Error instead of just logging console.warn
The error message includes the plugin name, expected hash, and actual hash
The existing console.warn is removed (the thrown error replaces it)
Promise.allSettled in the caller (injectPluginIntoMatchingTabs) correctly captures the rejection
No changes to the first-attempt or retry injection logic — only the post-retry failure path changes
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/iife-injection.ts lines 144-150. Current code logs a console.warn and continues. Replace lines 145-150 with: `if (!retryMatched) { const actualHash = await readAdapterHash(tabId, pluginName); throw new Error(`Adapter hash mismatch for ${pluginName} after retry: expected ${adapterHash}, got ${String(actualHash)}`); }`. The caller at line ~191 uses Promise.allSettled, so the rejection is safely captured and the tab is excluded from injectedTabIds. This is the correct architectural response — the caller already handles rejected promises.

userStories.title: Replace wall-clock modulo with elapsed-time tracking for E2E diagnostic collection
userStories.description: As a developer debugging E2E test failures, I want diagnostics to be collected reliably every ~2 seconds so that timeout errors always include useful diagnostic information.
acceptanceCriteria: The Date.now() % 2000 < 500 check on line 302 of helpers.ts is replaced with elapsed-time tracking
A variable like lastDiagnosticTime is initialized before the polling loop and updated when diagnostics are collected
Diagnostics are collected when Date.now() - lastDiagnosticTime >= 2000
The diagnostic collection logic itself is unchanged — only the sampling condition changes
userStories.notes: File: e2e/helpers.ts around line 302. The function is waitForAdapterInjection (or similar polling helper). Add `let lastDiagnosticTime = 0;` before the while loop. Replace `if (Date.now() % 2000 < 500)` with `const now = Date.now(); if (now - lastDiagnosticTime >= 2000)` and set `lastDiagnosticTime = now;` inside the block. This guarantees diagnostics are collected every 2 seconds of elapsed time regardless of wall-clock alignment.

userStories.title: Reject empty-string wsUrl from /ws-info endpoint in offscreen document
userStories.description: As a user with a misconfigured MCP server, I want a clear error when /ws-info returns an empty wsUrl so that I see an actionable message instead of a cryptic WebSocket connection loop.
acceptanceCriteria: The typeof check on line 298 of offscreen/index.ts also rejects empty strings
The condition becomes: typeof wsInfo.wsUrl === 'string' && wsInfo.wsUrl !== ''
When an empty wsUrl is received, a console.warn is logged explaining the server returned an empty URL
The fallback to rawUrl is used when wsUrl is empty (same as when wsUrl is missing)
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts line 298. Current: `if (typeof wsInfo.wsUrl === 'string')`. Change to: `if (typeof wsInfo.wsUrl === 'string' && wsInfo.wsUrl !== '')`. Add a console.warn in an else branch: `console.warn('[opentabs:offscreen] /ws-info returned empty wsUrl, using fallback URL');`. The same pattern should also be checked in background.ts around line 117 where the same /ws-info response is parsed — apply the same empty-string guard there too.

userStories.title: Log warning when critical sync notifications fail to send to extension
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want the MCP server to log when sendSyncFull or sendPluginUpdate fails to reach the extension so that I can diagnose extension desync issues from server logs.
acceptanceCriteria: sendSyncFull (around line 184 of extension-protocol.ts) checks the return value of sendToExtension and logs a warning if false
sendPluginUpdate (around line 294 of extension-protocol.ts) checks the return value and logs a warning if false
The log message includes which notification type failed (sync.full vs plugin.update)
Only a log.warn is added — no error throwing or retry logic (these are non-blocking notifications)
Other callers of sendToExtension that are responses to extension requests do NOT need the check (they are already in a connected context)
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts. For sendSyncFull (~line 184): wrap the sendToExtension call: `const sent = sendToExtension(state, { ... }); if (!sent) log.warn('Failed to send sync.full — extension not connected');`. Same pattern for sendPluginUpdate (~line 294): `const sent = sendToExtension(state, { ... }); if (!sent) log.warn('Failed to send plugin.update — extension not connected');`. The log object is already imported in this file. Do NOT change sendToExtension itself or add checks to response-type callers (those only fire when the extension is connected).
